data.title: Nintendo Stock (NTDOF) Drops on Reports of a New Switch 2 Variant
data.description:
Nintendo (NTDOF) stock dropped on Friday alongside reports that the video game company will release a new version of its Switch 2 console. According to these reports, Nintendo is getting ready to launch a version of the Switch 2 that will feature an easily replaceable battery.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The latest reports claim that Nintendo intends to release this version of the Switch 2 in Europe. The company would do so to comply with local "right to repair" laws that require easy fixes for common technology problems. A failing battery is among these issues, as it can be easily swapped out by owners for a new one.

Europe's move to make consumer repairs easier will affect more than just Nintendo. Several tech companies will have to redesign their products with the region in mind. One sector that is likely to be significantly changed by this is smartphones, which often don't have easy ways to replace degraded batteries. This could result in users hanging onto their smartphones for longer, changing upgrade cycles in the industry. This could be a negative for Samsung (SSNLF), Apple (AAPL), and Google (GOOGL).

Nintendo Stock Movement Today

Nintendo stock was down 3.95% on Friday, extending a 6.78% year-to-date fall. The shares have also decreased 15.32% over the past 12 months.

Trading activity for NTDOF stock was low today at about 1,390 shares, compared to a three-month average daily trading volume of about 9,700 shares.
data.author: William White

data.title: '#1 Pick for 2026,' Says Top Analyst About Amazon Stock (AMZN)
data.description:
Amazon (AMZN) remains a top pick for Evercore ISI, with the firm reiterating its bullish view after a deeper look at the company's cloud unit Amazon Web Services (AWS) growth and margins. Top analyst Mark Mahaney maintained an Outperform rating on the stock with a $285 price target, implying about 37% upside from current levels. He called Amazon his "#1 large-cap long idea for 2026."

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

AWS Growth and Margins Support the View

Evercore raised its estimates following a detailed review of AWS. The firm now expects AWS revenue to reach $163 billion in 2026, up 27% year-over-year, and $214 billion in 2027, with 31% growth. It also sees AWS operating margins at 34% in 2026 and 32% in 2027.

Along with this, Evercore increased its overall estimates for Amazon. Revenue and operating income forecasts were raised by 2–3% and now sit about 4–5% above Street expectations.

Why Evercore Calls Amazon a Top Pick

Mahaney sees several reasons for the positive view.

He points to Amazon's current valuation, noting the stock is trading near a three-year low on a P/E basis, which he views as attractive. He also expects a possible shift this year, with faster revenue growth and margin expansion.

In addition, Evercore sees upside from newer initiatives such as Project Leo and Perishable Checkout, which could start contributing more meaningfully in 2026.

Capex Remains a Key Watch Point

Spending remains the main concern. Evercore expects Amazon's capex to rise to around $250 billion by 2027, which could weigh on near-term free cash flow.

The firm estimates about $10 billion in free cash flow losses in both 2026 and 2027. It also noted that capex intensity could peak in 2026, though there is a chance it extends into 2027.

Even so, Evercore believes much of this risk is already reflected in the stock and says the overall risk-reward remains favorable.

Is Amazon a Buy, Sell, or Hold? 

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Strong Buy consensus rating on AMZN stock based on 40 Buys and three Holds assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average AMZN price target of $280.00 per share implies 34.13% upside potential.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: Dow Jones Slips as Trump Weighs Kharg Island Takeover
data.description:
The Dow Jones (DJIA) is set to open Friday lower following a report from Axios that President Trump is considering a takeover or blockade of Iran's Kharg Island as leverage to reopen the Strait of Hormuz.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

However, an operation would likely require the U.S. to send in ground troops. Last Friday, the U.S. struck the island with missiles as a warning, leaving oil infrastructure untouched. The island handles roughly 90% of Iran's oil exports via pipes from the mainland.

Forget margin or options. Here's how the pros trade QQQ

U.S. Eyes Kharg Takeover to Pressure Iran

Sources familiar with the matter said the U.S. would only consider a Kharg operation if it further weakens Iran's military capabilities around the strait. "We need about a month to weaken the Iranians more with strikes, take the island and then get them by the balls and use it for negotiations," said an anonymous source.

No final decision has been made yet, although experts warn that a takeover of the island wouldn't guarantee a reopening of the strait and would place U.S. troops in harm's way.
data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Fed's Bowman Backs Three Rate Cuts in 2026 to 'Support the Labor Market'
data.description:
Fed Vice Chair for Supervision Michelle Bowman reiterated her support for three interest rate cuts in 2026, saying that it's still too early to gauge the economic effects of the war in Iran.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

"It's too soon to tell what the impacts of Iran and the conflict may be, but I do expect that we'll start to see some of the supply-side policies working their way through the economy," she told Fox Business on Friday.

Have high conviction in QQQ? Leverage your view

Bowman Flags Jobs Weakness as Fed Holds Rates

Bowman added that she still has reservations about the labor market. In February, nonfarm payrolls dropped by 92,000, well below the consensus estimate of 50,000 additions, while the unemployment rate rose to 4.4% from 4.3%. Lower rates stimulate the labor market by reducing borrowing costs and encouraging businesses to invest and hire.

Earlier this week, the Fed voted 11-1 to maintain interest rates between 3.50% and 3.75%. Fed Governor Stephen Miran dissented in favor of a 25 bps rate cut.
data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Intuitive Machines (LUNR) Stock Scores Price Target Increases from Top Wall Street Analysts
data.description:
Intuitive Machines (LUNR) stock was down on Friday even after the company scored a handful of price target increases from analysts following its most recent earnings report.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Let's check out the latest analyst price target updates for LUNR stock below:

  • Five-star Roth MKM analyst Sujeeva De Silva reiterated a Buy rating and boosted his price target to $25 from $16, representing a potential 32.21% upside.
  • Four-star Canaccord Genuity analyst Austin Moeller maintained a Buy rating and raised his price target to $24 from $22.50, suggesting a 26.92% upside. Moeller said he expects the company to grow and add new programs with its purchase of Lanteris.
  • Four-star Cantor Fitzgerald analyst Andres Sheppard kept a Buy rating and increased his price target to $26 from $16, implying a 37.49% upside. Sheppard argued that Intuitive Machines' purchase of Lanteris was not only synergistic but also additive to the bottom line.

Intuitive Machines' latest earnings report was a win for the company. While its revenue was below estimates, the company significantly reduced its net loss on a year-over-year basis. The company also provided 2026 guidance that was above Wall Street's estimates.

Intuitive Machines Stock Movement Today

Intuitive Machines stock slipped 0.9% in pre-market trading on Friday, following a 4.42% rally yesterday. The shares have rallied 16.51% year-to-date and 166.71% over the past 12 months.

Trading activity for LUNR stock today was muted, with more than 179,000 shares exchanged, compared to the company's three-month average daily trading volume of about 9.54 million.
data.author: William White

data.title: Salesforce Stock (CRM) Stays Strong in the SaaSpocalypse
data.description:
Salesforce (CRM) stock continues to show resilience in the ongoing SaaSpocalypse, even as the broader software sector remains under pressure. While the artificial intelligence (AI) disruption narrative has clearly intensified across software, the broad-based nature of the decline suggests this is much more of a generalized de-rating than a company-specific breakdown. From that perspective, whether the market is accurately pricing Salesforce's AI exposure — or simply extrapolating a worst-case scenario across the entire sector — I think there's an asymmetry in the setup, especially with the stock now trading in what looks like deep-value territory.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Over the long run, though, I'd expect that asymmetry to correct, which is why I'm bullish on Salesforce.

Why SaaS Is Being Repriced Across the Board

SaaS companies have been living through something close to pandemonium over at least the past six months, and Salesforce has been no exception. When we look at the recent performance of the top 10 holdings in the iShares Expanded Tech-Software Sector ETF (IGV), it's clear that CRM has seen a sharp devaluation of nearly a quarter of its market cap, broadly in line with its sector peers.
data.author: Bernard Zambonin

data.title: 'Don't Give Up on Gold!' Says Analyst Who Has Studied 50 Years of Precious Metal Bull Runs
data.description:
The gold bull run of the last 12 months has been blasted by the Iranian war, but investors are being urged to read their history and not give up on the precious metal yet.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Gold Blasted By Iran War

The spot gold price has rocketed 60% higher over the last 12 months driven by investors flocking to it as a safe haven during a number of geopolitical and economic crises. Falling interest rates, which are good for gold, a weaker dollar and central bank demand have also played their part.

But, it's another geopolitical crisis – the current Iran war – which has seen a big retreat in the price. It has dropped 5% over the last month falling below the magical $5,000 an ounce level.
data.author: David Craik

data.title: Why Is Vanguard Total Stock Market ETF (VTI) Down Today, 3/20/26?
data.description:
The Vanguard Total Stock Market ETF (VTI), which tracks the broader U.S. market, is down 0.43% in pre-market trading on Thursday, continuing its decline from the previous session. The drop follows a more than 3% fall in Tesla (TSLA) stock yesterday, after the NHTSA expanded its investigation into the company's FSD program. TSLA is among VTI's top 10 holdings, which adds pressure to the ETF. Meanwhile, investor sentiment has also weakened as U.S. markets remain volatile amid the ongoing conflict in Iran and rising oil prices — with the S&P 500 (SPX) and Nasdaq both falling in pre‑market trading.
